name: Post-FX Flash
description: Triggers a time-limited post-processing flash (film grain, chromatic aberration, or LUT intensity) in response to EventBus events. Useful for gameplay feedback like damage flashes, warp/teleport effects, and ability activations.
author: stemstudio

triggerEvent.description: EventBus topic that triggers the flash (e.g. game.health.dec, enemy.got.hit, custom.warp.activated).

pass.description: Which post-processing pass to flash. The pass must be enabled in Project Settings > Post-Processing for the flash to be visible.

duration.description: Total flash duration in seconds (attack + hold + decay).

peakIntensity.description: Maximum uniform value the pass reaches at peak. Typical ranges: film/lut 0.5-1.0, chromaticAberration 0.02-0.05.

documentation:
## Post-FX Flash

Bridges EventBus gameplay events to time-limited post-processing flashes. Each time the configured `triggerEvent` fires, the behavior kicks a short flash on the selected pass — landing the uniform back on whatever baseline value the user has set in Project Settings.

### How It Works
1. On behavior add, subscribes to `attributes.triggerEvent` on the global EventBus.
2. When the event fires, calls `effectRenderer.flashPass(pass, duration, peakIntensity)`.
3. The renderer drives the uniform through a linear attack/hold/decay envelope over `duration` seconds, then restores the baseline.
4. Overlapping flashes on the same pass replace the previous one (do not stack).

### Gameplay Use Cases
- **Damage feedback**: subscribe to `game.health.dec` → chromatic aberration spike.
- **Teleport/warp**: custom event from ability behavior → film grain flash + CA.
- **Power-up activation**: custom event → LUT intensity momentarily boosted for a stylistic shift.
- **Enemy hit feedback** (when playing as the enemy): subscribe to `enemy.got.hit`.

### Requirements
- The target pass MUST be enabled in Project Settings > Rendering > Post-Processing. The behavior does NOT force-enable a disabled pass (that would cause a pipeline rebuild mid-gameplay, which is visually jarring).
- Works on WebGPU renderer. On WebGL fallback, the underlying TSL passes may not be available and flashes will be no-ops.

### Manual Trigger
Gameplay code can call `.flash()` directly instead of firing an EventBus event:
```ts
const postFx = this.game.getBehavior(target, 'postFx');
postFx?.flash();                              // use attribute-configured defaults
postFx?.flash('film', 0.5, 0.8);              // override
```

### Attributes
- `triggerEvent`: EventBus topic to subscribe to (e.g. `game.health.dec`).
- `pass`: one of `film`, `chromaticAberration`, `lut`.
- `duration`: total flash time in seconds.
- `peakIntensity`: maximum uniform value reached at peak.

### Best Practices
- Short durations (0.15–0.4s) for hit feedback — long flashes feel laggy.
- Subtle peak intensities for chromatic aberration (0.02–0.05); above 0.1 becomes distracting.
- For continuous ambient effects (always-on film grain, ambient LUT), use Project Settings directly — flashing is for moment-to-moment feedback.
- Attach to a persistent object (scene root, player) so the subscription survives object lifecycle.
